jokes.text: We got an hour and 23 minutes till showtime, which explains the bad timing of your jokes.
jokes.setup: Philip mentions timing concerns about the show
jokes.punchline: Artie connects show timing to Philip's joke timing
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that reveals character dynamics and gets a laugh through misdirection - classic Artie wit

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: We got an hour and 23 minutes till showtime, which explains the bad timing of your jokes.

jokes.text: I hope Larry's back doesn't fall off. Because then, what would hold up his ass?
jokes.punchline: Anatomical impossibility used to insult Larry
jokes.explanation: Clever anatomical joke with good structure and surprising punchline

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I hope Larry's back doesn't fall off. Because then, what would hold up his ass?

jokes.text: Well, if Larry's not going to rehearse, I don't think I should be forced to rehearse.
jokes.setup: Artie tells Hank they're rehearsing without Larry
jokes.punchline: Hank's petulant, illogical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - childish and self-important, very character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, if Larry's not going to rehearse, I don't think I should be forced to rehearse.

jokes.text: Larry manipulating a doctor into prescribing more pills by escalating his 'pain'
jokes.setup: Larry calls doctor about tooth pain
jokes.punchline: Systematic manipulation from 10 to 15 to 20 pills
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's addiction through believable manipulation - very uncomfortable and real

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Doctor, I'm in terrible pain. I need something strong.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: How bad is the pain on a scale of 1 to 10?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's a 15.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: That's higher than 10. I can prescribe you 10 pills.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: 10 pills? That won't be enough. The pain is actually a 20.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: A 20? Then I'll prescribe you 20 pills.

jokes.text: That's funny. You should have your own talk show.
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggests doctor should host a talk show
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the talk show world, delivered with Larry's characteristic sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's funny. You should have your own talk show.

jokes.text: You're up to 3 of those now? That is the recommended dosage. Yeah, for a 1,500-pound man.
jokes.punchline: Beverly's comeback about the excessive dosage
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that highlights Larry's pill abuse through exaggeration

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: You're up to 3 of those now?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That is the recommended dosage.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Yeah, for a 1,500-pound man.

jokes.text: Oh, well, I've never been so insulted in my life.
jokes.setup: Beverly calls him essentially obese through the dosage comment
jokes.punchline: Larry's mock offense at being called heavy
jokes.explanation: Larry's vanity showing through - character-appropriate but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I've never been so insulted in my life.

jokes.text: They already filled it up 5 times, so obviously they're lying
jokes.setup: Pharmacy won't fill prescription more than 3 times
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic that 5 fillings prove they're lying about the 3-time limit
jokes.explanation: Perfect addict logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with conviction

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They already filled it up 5 times, so obviously they're lying

jokes.text: Clinton's right. The health care system in this country is just all fucked.
jokes.punchline: Blames healthcare system for his drug-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflection - using political talking points to justify addiction, very 90s-specific

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Clinton's right. The health care system in this country is just all fucked.

jokes.text: I told props that I needed a flammable animal.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre request for flammable animal prop
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist humor typical of behind-the-scenes chaos - very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I told props that I needed a flammable animal.

jokes.text: Larry, right on time as usual.
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic comment about Larry's punctuality
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm, predictable but character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Larry, right on time as usual.

jokes.text: Alex Trebek? Alex Trebek. No. I'm just saying...
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about standing up during the show
jokes.punchline: Random comparison to Alex Trebek's standing hosting style
jokes.explanation: Larry's drug-addled brain making random connections - shows his mental state

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Alex Trebek? Alex Trebek. No. I'm just saying...

jokes.text: Maybe it's mama bear.
jokes.setup: Someone has been sitting in Larry's chair
jokes.punchline: Reference to Goldilocks story
jokes.explanation: Fairy tale reference in mundane context - becomes important callback later

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe it's mama bear.

jokes.text: Talentless fat fuck.
jokes.punchline: Harsh insult directed at Hank
jokes.explanation: Brutal insult that becomes a major plot point - shocking impact drives much of episode

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Talentless fat fuck.

jokes.text: I'm going to do the show in my car where the seats are adjusted properly.
jokes.punchline: Threatens to host show from his car
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation showing Larry's drug-fueled lack of perspective

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm going to do the show in my car where the seats are adjusted properly.

jokes.text: What a delightful bride of Christ you make, Roseanne.
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic religious reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - sarcastic and culturally literate, plays off Roseanne's image

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What a delightful bride of Christ you make, Roseanne.

jokes.text: This place looks like the stock room at Upjohn.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to pharmaceutical company warehouse
jokes.explanation: Sharp pharmaceutical reference that perfectly captures the scope of Larry's problem

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: This place looks like the stock room at Upjohn.

jokes.text: These are placebos. Part of that modified rationing program I told you about.
jokes.setup: Pills found with Artie's name on them
jokes.punchline: Artie's elaborate excuse about placebos
jokes.explanation: Artie's creative excuse-making shows his enabling behavior

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: These are placebos. Part of that modified rationing program I told you about.

jokes.text: He told me root canal. OK, so I'm not an expert in periodontics. Are you?
jokes.setup: Artie caught in contradiction about dental work
jokes.punchline: Defensive deflection with fake expertise
jokes.explanation: Artie digging deeper hole with pseudo-medical terminology

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: He told me root canal. OK, so I'm not an expert in periodontics. Are you?

jokes.text: Bottle of Pernacet half-empty. Or half-full, depending on how you look at it.
jokes.punchline: Optimism cliché applied to pill bottle
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on optimistic cliché - perfectly captures the grim situation

dialogue.line: Bottle of Pernacet half-empty. Or half-full, depending on how you look at it.

jokes.text: Who do you think I am, huh? Patty Hearst?
jokes.setup: Being told he can't leave his house
jokes.punchline: Comparison to famous kidnapping victim
jokes.explanation: Larry's dramatic self-victimization with cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Who do you think I am, huh? Patty Hearst?

jokes.text: Fox will make a TV movie about you before the end of the year.
jokes.punchline: Fox's exploitation of celebrity scandals
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire - very prescient about tabloid TV movies

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fox will make a TV movie about you before the end of the year.

jokes.text: Martin Mull. Threatens Larry. Mike Myers. Threatens Larry. Tony Danza. Threatens Larry. Pat Sajak.
jokes.punchline: Every comedian threatens Larry except Pat Sajak
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's paranoia while setting up Pat Sajak as the 'safe' choice

dialogue.line: Martin Mull. Threatens Larry. Mike Myers. Threatens Larry. Tony Danza. Threatens Larry. Pat Sajak.

jokes.text: They made me book him 'cause he's the only one who doesn't threaten Larry.
jokes.punchline: Confirms he was chosen for being unthreatening
jokes.explanation: Pays off the 'threatens Larry' setup while explaining casting logic

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: They made me book him 'cause he's the only one who doesn't threaten Larry.

jokes.text: What's rule number 2, filling his prescriptions as soon as possible?
jokes.setup: Beverly says job rule #1 is keeping Larry's secrets
jokes.punchline: Artie suggests rule #2 is enabling drug addiction
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback to Beverly's enabling behavior. Good structure with perfect punchline timing.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What's rule number 2, filling his prescriptions as soon as possible?

jokes.text: 'Talentless fat fuck,' right? I catch onto things quickly. Yes. Very hurtful. Not entirely accurate. I've lost upwards of 14 pounds.
jokes.punchline: Only disputes the 'fat' part, not 'talentless'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank response - missing the point entirely while focusing on weight loss

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: 'Talentless fat fuck,' right? I catch onto things quickly. Yes. Very hurtful. Not entirely accurate. I've lost upwards of 14 pounds.

jokes.text: Oh, so... so 'talentless fuck' would have been more appropriate. You skinny little shit.
jokes.punchline: Artie's logic and Hank's counter-insult
jokes.explanation: Great payoff with Artie's brutal logic and Hank's defensive response

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, so... so 'talentless fuck' would have been more appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You skinny little shit.

jokes.text: A bad case of Pat Sajakitis. We're all giddy with it, my boy.
jokes.punchline: Made-up disease named after guest host
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that dismisses inquiry while mocking the situation

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: A bad case of Pat Sajakitis. We're all giddy with it, my boy.

jokes.text: When did Sajak get so fucking funny?
jokes.setup: Watching Pat Sajak perform magic trick
jokes.punchline: Artie's surprise at Sajak's entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Shows business surprise at unexpected talent from game show host

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: When did Sajak get so fucking funny?

jokes.text: No. I have absolutely no idea. I see. So your show's still in the top 10, huh? Well, yeah. Until they run that Frasier guy against us.
jokes.punchline: Artie's sarcastic dismissal followed by Larry's competitive anxiety
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's competitive insecurity even while making excuses

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. I have absolutely no idea. I see.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So your show's still in the top 10, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Until they run that Frasier guy against us.

jokes.text: You don't think Frasier's that good, do you?
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about his drug problem
jokes.punchline: Larry's anxious deflection to ratings concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - even in crisis, obsessed with competition and validation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You don't think Frasier's that good, do you?

jokes.text: That's French for get 'em while they're hot!
jokes.setup: Beverly says 'c'est la vie' about microwaved food
jokes.punchline: Wrong translation of French phrase
jokes.explanation: Beverly's misunderstanding of French shows her character's quirks

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: That's French for get 'em while they're hot!

jokes.text: It's like a wine cellar with handles.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to wine collection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that captures both the collection aspect and addiction elegance

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: It's like a wine cellar with handles.

jokes.text: You forced me to talk to my agent without a pill. Are you aware of that?
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror at facing his agent sober
jokes.explanation: Perfect show business joke - agents are so awful you need drugs to deal with them

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You forced me to talk to my agent without a pill. Are you aware of that?

jokes.text: Did you pull her out of a burning building at some point, or did you sit through She Devil?
jokes.setup: Asking why Roseanne is helping Larry
jokes.punchline: Two explanations: heroism or enduring bad movie
jokes.explanation: Great agent humor - equating physical rescue with professional loyalty through bad projects

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Did you pull her out of a burning building at some point, or did you sit through She Devil?

jokes.text: She's with William Morris, right?
jokes.punchline: Agent immediately pivots to business concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect agent behavior - can't help but see everything as business opportunity

dialogue.line: She's with William Morris, right?

jokes.text: Larry, if it's any consolation, I pulled my groin once, too, and I know how painful that can be. I heard he had laryngitis. Well, you know, when you pull your groin, your voice has a tendency to go. Yeah. Go up at least, I guess.
jokes.punchline: Absurd medical connection between groin and voice
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of fake medical excuses with voice pitch implication

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Cesario
dialogue.line: Larry, if it's any consolation, I pulled my groin once, too, and I know how painful that can be. I heard he had laryngitis. Well, you know, when you pull your groin, your voice has a tendency to go. Yeah. Go up at least, I guess.

jokes.text: Jamie Lee Curtis has never been on the show, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he was funny with Jamie Lee Curtis
jokes.punchline: Artie's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Devastating reality check showing Larry's drug-induced delusions

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Jamie Lee Curtis has never been on the show, Larry.

jokes.text: When you were smoking weed, you were kinda funny. But when you started taking these percs and bourbon, it's like you're working behind 8 feet of ice!
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor about performance impediment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that perfectly captures the difference between drugs and their performance effects

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: When you were smoking weed, you were kinda funny. But when you started taking these percs and bourbon, it's like you're working behind 8 feet of ice!

jokes.text: Hank thinks it's the Santa Ana winds. He said Larry's allergic and his face swells up.
jokes.punchline: Hank's ridiculous weather-based theory
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank pseudo-medical explanation showing his character's know-it-all tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: Hank thinks it's the Santa Ana winds. He said Larry's allergic and his face swells up.

jokes.text: It's a fucking tic-tac. Oh... but that's progress.
jokes.setup: Larry dramatically discarding what he thinks is a pill
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's just candy, Roseanne's encouraging response
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Larry's paranoia and Roseanne's supportive nature

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's a fucking tic-tac.

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: Oh... but that's progress.

jokes.text: I didn't have a drug problem. I was lying because I was covering a nervous breakdown. Number 2, it was on television, so it doesn't mean shit.
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic about TV vs. reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV reality while showing Larry's compartmentalization

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I didn't have a drug problem. I was lying because I was covering a nervous breakdown. Number 2, it was on television, so it doesn't mean shit.

jokes.text: They go through my garbage.
jokes.setup: Asked if he tells his staff everything
jokes.punchline: Staff discovers things by going through trash
jokes.explanation: Reveals the invasive nature of show business relationships

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They go through my garbage.

jokes.text: Yeah, but prescription nonetheless. I think... they're ones that anybody could get or probably did. Anybody takes... addicts.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to minimize his drug problem
jokes.punchline: Verbal fumbling that accidentally admits to addiction
jokes.explanation: Larry's verbal stumbling reveals truth despite his attempts to minimize

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, but prescription nonetheless. I think... they're ones that anybody could get or probably did. Anybody takes... addicts.

jokes.text: Pay up, everybody. So, Paula, you, uh... Paula, how much did you win? $200. So you bet that I had a drug problem. Yes, sir. Well, consider that a Christmas bonus.
jokes.setup: Staff had betting pool on Larry's problem
jokes.punchline: Larry treating gambling winnings as bonus
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about staff exploitation of boss's problems

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Pay up, everybody. So, Paula, you, uh... Paula, how much did you win?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: $200. So you bet that I had a drug problem. Yes, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, consider that a Christmas bonus.

jokes.text: I believe that's 190 more than you received last year.
jokes.setup: Paula won $200 betting on his addiction
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals how little he paid her last Christmas
jokes.explanation: Reveals Larry's cheapness while trying to be generous

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I believe that's 190 more than you received last year.

jokes.text: It hurt me when you rejected my gay evangelist sketch. Oh, come on. That sketch was over the top. There was a character named Jerry Fagwell in it.
jokes.punchline: Larry's justified criticism of offensive character name
jokes.explanation: Shows writer room dynamics and Larry's editorial judgment

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: It hurt me when you rejected my gay evangelist sketch.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, come on. That sketch was over the top. There was a character named Jerry Fagwell in it.

jokes.text: I'm not the one with the pill problem, you self-destructive asshole.
jokes.punchline: Phil's defensive explosion
jokes.explanation: Good intervention breakdown - shows how these sessions can backfire

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I'm not the one with the pill problem, you self-destructive asshole.

jokes.text: Oh. You were just so doped out on drugs, you just... you didn't know what you were saying. Hank, that's water under the bridge. I remember, though, exactly what I was saying.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to forgive Larry's insult
jokes.punchline: Larry confirming he meant the insult
jokes.explanation: Brutal callback - Larry crushes Hank's attempt at forgiveness

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh. You were just so doped out on drugs, you just... you didn't know what you were saying.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hank, that's water under the bridge. I remember, though, exactly what I was saying.

jokes.text: Well, are you sure that you're not just saying that because you want the time slot after my show? I don't think so. I don't really want your time slot.
jokes.punchline: Roseanne's business-minded response
jokes.explanation: Perfect show business joke about marriage proposals - everything is about career advancement

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: Well, are you sure that you're not just saying that because you want the time slot after my show?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't really want your time slot.

jokes.text: I want you. Oh, jeez. Say that again. I want you. No. The thing about the time slot.
jokes.punchline: Roseanne more interested in business implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Roseanne's priorities are hilariously business-focused even in romance

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I want you.

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: Oh, jeez. Say that again.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I want you.

dialogue.speaker: Roseanne
dialogue.line: No. The thing about the time slot.
